tweet: What are some in the Puerto Rico Left seeking with this? To make it a new Constitutional Convention w/o going through the constitutionally established process? What for? To abridge people’s rights in the new status, to socialize private property & enterprise?

tweet: For more information on hurricane season preparedness visit  https://t.co/DXKmCgUBWO.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • Download the FEMA app to receive weather alerts.  Now is the time to be prepared!  If you or someone in your family is experiencing issues with disaster-related stress, call Línea PAS at 800-981-0023, TTY 888-672-7622.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: STAY INFORMED: Listen for up-to-date information  • Local media will provide evacuation and shelters details. • Make sure your battery-powered radio is working and you have extra batteries.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • Save a few dollars each week and spread out your shopping for your 10 days’ worth of supplies. • Switch your Social Security or other regular benefits to electronic payments that are deposited directly into your account.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • Whether you’re a homeowner or renter, contact your insurance agent to ensure you’re adequately covered for housing, car and flooding. • Keep some cash in hand, in large and small bills.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • In mass care shelters, pets will be taken to a reserved space. Certified service pets are allowed to stay with their owners for emotional support.  BE FINANCIALLY PREPARED: Consider the costs associated with disasters • Anticipate out-of-pocket expenses for lodging, food, gas

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: PROTECT YOUR PETS: Make sure someone is available to care of your pets if you can’t • Get food, water and a collar with identification tag. • Keep pets inside the house or take them to a pre-identified safe location.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • If relying on public transportation, contact your local emergency agencies about evacuation procedures. • If you plan to stay at home, make sure it is in good repair and have the plywood, steel or aluminum panels to board up windows and doors.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: • Keep important phone numbers with you at all times.  ASSESS YOUR SHELTERING NEEDS: Identify where you will shelter  • Identify where you will shelter in case you are ordered to leave your home, and share the location with your family.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: MAKE A FAMILY PLAN: Identify alternate ways to stay in touch with loved ones  • Choose an out of town friend or relative as a point of contact. • Decide on a meeting place in case you cannot return home.

name: Jenniffer González
tweet: BUILD A KIT: Be sure you have at least 10 days of supplies • Store a gallon of water, food, prescriptions and other supplies per person per day. Pay special attention to infants, the elderly and people with disabilities and access and functional needs.
